title: AST Unattended Death: Big Lake [AK25088476]
summary: On 09/01/2025 Alaska State Troopers responded to a residence in Big Lake following the report of an unattended death. Investigation revealed James Belcher, age 83, of Big Lake, had died of natural causes. Next of kin was notified and no foul play is suspected.

title: With gas crunch looming, Alaska utilities won’t get big wind before tax credits expire
summary: Wind turbines turn in Kodiak on May 14, 2018. (Photo by Dennis Schroeder/NREL) For years, urban Alaska utilities have been studying large-scale wind farms that could help break the state’s dependence on natural gas power — encouraged by the potential for hundreds of millions of dollars in tax credits from the federal government. Next summer, however, those tax credits will largely disappear for projects that haven’t started construction, a consequence of the tax bill that President Donald Trump signed in July. Clean energy advocates, and U.S. Sen. Lisa Murkowski, had said they hoped that Alaska wind projects could still advance in time to qualify. But in recent weeks, board members and executives at the cooperatively owned utilities have acknowledged that the timeline now appears too short — which means any large-scale projects will now have to be built without the generous federal subsidies, or wait to see if Congress reestablishes a more favorable tax regime. Critics say the absence...

title: Monday, September 1, 2025
summary: Summer is prime time for gathering and baking camas across the Pacific Northwest, Rockies, and Plains regions. Native people usually harvest – then bake – the plant’s bulbs, as part of their First Foods menu. As Brian Bull (Nez Perce) of Buffalo’s Fire reports, there was even a “bake-off” held outside Eugene, Oreg. recently.
